title: Casuarina Wood (Casuarina equisetifolia): Properties, Density & Uses
summary: Casuarina (Casuarina equisetifolia): heavy, dense coastal hardwood at ~900-1000 kg/m3 - density, hardness, seasoning, durability and its packing-timber uses.

title: Subabul Wood (Leucaena leucocephala): Properties, Density & Uses
summary: Subabul (Leucaena leucocephala): medium-density plantation wood (~500-650 kg/m3) that seasons well and treats easily — for packing cases, pallets and ply core.

title: Anjili (Wild Jack) Wood (Artocarpus hirsutus): Properties, Density & Uses
summary: Anjili (Wild Jack, Artocarpus hirsutus): a Western Ghats hardwood with teak-class strength (~600 kg/m3), very high durability, ideal for packing cases.

title: Matti (Indian Laurel) Wood (Terminalia elliptica): Properties, Density & Uses
summary: Matti (Indian laurel, Terminalia elliptica): a heavy, hard 800-870 kg/m3 hardwood for heavy-duty packing cases, pallets, sawn timber. Uses, density, sourcing.

title: Irul (Pyinkado) Wood (Xylia xylocarpa): Properties, Density & Uses
summary: Irul (Pyinkado / Burma Ironwood, Xylia xylocarpa) is one of India's heaviest, most durable hardwoods - its properties, density, working traits and packing uses.

title: Venteak Wood (Lagerstroemia microcarpa): Properties, Density & Uses
summary: Venteak (benteak, Lagerstroemia microcarpa) is a Western Ghats teak substitute at ~640 kg/m3. Properties, density, seasoning, durability and packing uses.

title: Kadam Wood (Neolamarckia cadamba): Properties, Density & Uses
summary: Kadam (Neolamarckia cadamba): a light, fast-growing Indian plantation hardwood for plywood core veneer and packing cases. Density, strength, durability & uses.

title: Pala (White Cheesewood) Wood (Alstonia scholaris): Properties, Density & Uses
summary: Pala (white cheesewood, Alstonia scholaris) is a light, soft, low-density wood for packing cases, match splints and plywood core. Density, durability & uses.
